scenario: At Aurora Telco, a junior modeller has 90 minutes to draft a DMN decision in Camunda 8.8 Web Modeler and then morph the top decision into a decision table to begin editing the pricing rules for 3000 customers on the new tariff plan.
question: Where in Camunda 8.8 Web Modeler does the modeller start, and how does she begin editing the morphed decision table?

options.text: Start in the decision table view, drag a decision from the right-side palette onto the diagram, then double-click the decision header in the properties panel to begin editing the rules
Start in the Decision Requirements Diagram view, drag a decision from the right-side palette onto the diagram, then click the overlay on the lower right corner of the morphed decision
Start in the decision table view, drag a decision from the left-side palette onto the diagram, then click the green icon in the lower left corner of the decision to begin editing the rules
Start in the Decision Requirements Diagram view, drag a decision from the left-side palette onto the diagram, then click the overlay on the upper left corner of the morphed decision

a.text: Incorrect. The Start modeling section says modelling starts in the DRD view rather than in the decision table view, and the palette sits on the left side of the canvas rather than the right.

b.text: Incorrect. Although modelling does start in the DRD view, the overlay that opens the decision table sits on the upper left corner of the morphed decision rather than the lower right corner.

c.text: Incorrect. Modelling starts in the DRD view rather than the decision table view, and the page describes an overlay on the upper left corner rather than a green icon in the lower left corner.

d.text: Correct. The page says modelling starts in the DRD view with elements added from the left-side palette by drag and drop, and that clicking the overlay on the upper left corner of the morphed decision opens table editing.

explanation: The Start modeling section anchors the workflow to the Decision Requirements Diagram view, with a left-side palette for adding elements by drag and drop and a clickable overlay on the upper left corner of a morphed decision to begin editing the table.
